header.note: We are in the process of moving strings from Bloom.xlf to BloomMediumPriority.xlf and BloomLowPriority.xlf while trying very hard to preserve translations and approval status.

trans-unit.note: This checkbox enables the experimental publish-to-apps workflow.

trans-unit.source: You can use elevenlabs.io to create sound effects if your book is non-commercial. Make sure to give credit to “elevenlabs.io”.

trans-unit.note: Label of a button that puts the Bloom Game tool into its the mode for adding elements that should show when the reader gets the answer right.

trans-unit.source: Add items that will show when the learner gets the correct answer. For example, you might add a text that says “Correct!” with a GIF of a dancing chicken.
During game play, this item can be moved around.
Place the targets in the places needed for the correct answer.

trans-unit.note: This is the label of an item that can be dragged onto the canvas to hold text, typically a single letter. It works best if it is one of the widest letters (including digraphs) in your alphabet
Used as the label of a button that is otherwise the name of a sound file, indicating that no sound has been chosen.

trans-unit.source: Untick this item if it is not part of the right answer
This item comes with a “target” to which it must be dragged. If you want to have more items than targets, turn this off for some of them.

trans-unit.note: Label of a button that puts the Bloom Game tool into its the mode for playing (testing) the game.
Used as the label of a button that is otherwise the name of a sound file, indicating that no sound has been chosen.

trans-unit.source: Keep all targets the same size so students can't use size as a clue.
Type a sentence. The reader will be asked to sort the words.

trans-unit.note: Label of a button that puts the Bloom Game tool into its initial mode for setting up the game.
This is the label of an item that can be dragged onto the canvas to hold text, typically about the length of a word.
Label of a button that puts the Bloom Game tool into its the mode for adding elements that should show when the reader gets the answer wrong.

trans-unit.source: Add items that will show when the learner gets the wrong answer. For example, you might add a text that says “Try Again!”.\nIt is OK to have no correct or wrong answers. Just give the same positive reinforcement, like “Looks Nice!”.
Drag the letters where they go
Drag the images where they go
Drag the images to their shadows
Drag the correct sound to matching word
Drag the word to the matching image
Match each image to a word
Drag the words where they go

trans-unit.note: Obsolete as of Bloom 6.3, though we might reinstate it

trans-unit.source: Check the box next to each correct answer.
Place the correct image in the highlighted button, put other images in the other buttons. When it is time to play, Bloom will shuffle the order of buttons.
Type the correct word in the highlighted button, put other words in the other buttons. When it is time to play, Bloom will shuffle the order of buttons.

trans-unit.note: This text is shown if an error occurred while running eSpeak. eSpeak is a piece of software that this program uses to do text-to-speech (have the computer read text out loud).

trans-unit.source: Please split or clear the existing recording in this text box before changing modes.
trans-unit.note: Shown if you open TalkingBookTool, enable "Record the whole text box...", make a recording, and then attempt to uncheck the "Record the whole text box..." input (without first clearing the audio recordings you are about to lose)
For "split", please use same terminology as in ID: EditTab.Toolbox.TalkingBookTool.SplitLabel
For "clear", please use same terminology as in ID: EditTab.Toolbox.TalkingBookTool.Clear
Shown if you open TalkingBookTool and then open the 'Advanced' subsection

trans-unit.source: If you import this recording, it will replace all the audio for this text box.

trans-unit.note: A segment marker is just "|" and it is used to break a sentence up into smaller sections for highlighting in sync with the audio.
Shown if you open TalkingBookTool and then open the 'Advanced' subsection

A timings file is either from Audacity or generated by Bloom. It contains one line for each segment of the text. The line contains the start and stop time of the audio that should be synchronized with that text segment.
Shown if you open TalkingBookTool and then open the 'Advanced' subsection
Shown if you open TalkingBookTool and then open the 'Advanced' subsection

trans-unit.source: Use an external tool like Audacity. When you're done, use "Apply Timings File..."
trans-unit.note: Shown if you open TalkingBookTool and then open the 'Advanced' subsection

trans-unit.source: If Bloom's timing guesses based on pauses aren't accurate enough, you can use this more advanced tool instead. Keep in mind that this will erase any changes you've made. Check [here] for more details.
trans-unit.note: The part inside square brackets will become a link.

trans-unit.source: This will remove any adjustments you have made.

trans-unit.note: Title of the dialog launched by the previous command. Usually they differ only by leaving out the ...

trans-unit.source: This is disabled because there is not a whole text box recording

Click this to insert a "|" character into the text at the current cursor position. This character tells Bloom to introduce a new segment for the purpose of highlighting the text during audio playback. You can also just type the "|" character using your keyboard.
Bloom will hide these when you publish.
trans-unit.note: This will be appended to the end of EditTab.Toolbox.TalkingBookTool.InsertSegmentMarkerTip. "these" refers to the "|" characters that mark audio segments, described in EditTab.Toolbox.TalkingBookTool.InsertSegmentMarkerTip

trans-unit.source: First, put the cursor in a box which has something to record.
trans-unit.note: This is shown on a button that only makes sense if the cursor is in a box of text.

trans-unit.source: If you want to change this mode, first use the "Clear" button to remove your recording.
Record one sentence at a time. You can break up sentences using the `|` character.
Record the whole text box in one take, or load in a recording.
Import an mp3 recording of the whole text box.
This is disabled because this box is not in `Record by Text Box` mode.
Open the timing file in a text editor. After you save your changes, click the "Apply Timings File" button.
This is only enabled after doing a "Split" of a recording of the whole text box.

This lets you choose a file containing timings for aligning the audio with the text. The format of the file is tab-separated values, with the first column being the start time in seconds, and the second column being the end time in seconds. The third column is often a label, but it will be ignored.
Control the order in which various boxes on the page are played back.

trans-unit.note: Shown if you open TalkingBookTool and then open the 'Advanced' subsection
Image descriptions are typed out, then recorded, so that the book can be used by the blind. This is a switch that shows the typed out image descriptions next to each image on the page.
Shown if you open TalkingBookTool and then open the 'Advanced' subsection

trans-unit.source: A grid of buttons that jump you to books in your app
Drag any of these onto a canvas:

trans-unit.note: Leave a margin around the image within the button.
Fill up the button with the image, leaving a margin on the sides as needed.
Fill up the button with the image, leaving no margin, cropping if necessary
Button text for opening the dialog to choose books for a link grid

trans-unit.source: A button that jumps you to a page or book in your app

trans-unit.note: Label for an expandable section in the Canvas Tool that contains navigation-related objects (e.g. buttons and book grids) that can be dragged onto a page.
Default text for a navigation button
Used in the Key hints at the bottom of the Canvas Tool for Alt+drag and Ctrl+drag.
Used in the Key hint at the bottom of the Canvas Tool for "Alt + drag = resize".
Used in the Key hint at the bottom of the Canvas Tool for "Ctrl + drag = move".

trans-unit.source: Select this if you want readers to be able to choose to read the book in this language.
trans-unit.note: This is a tooltip on a checkbox. The checkbox contains the name of a language. The checkbox controls whether this language will be included in the published book.

trans-unit.source: This is disabled because this language is currently shown in the book, so it is required.
trans-unit.note: This is a tooltip on a checkbox. The checkbox contains the name of a language. The checkbox controls whether this language will be included in the published book. In this case, the user is not allowed to change the value.

trans-unit.source: Select this if you want to include this audio.
trans-unit.note: This is a tooltip on a checkbox. The checkbox contains the name of a language. The checkbox controls whether the Talking Book audio for this language will be included in the published book.

trans-unit.source: This is disabled because this book does not have any audio in this language.
trans-unit.note: This is a tooltip on a checkbox. The checkbox contains the name of a language. The checkbox controls whether the Talking Book audio for this language will be included in the published book. In this case, the user is not allowed to change the value.

trans-unit.source: This book does not have any interactive activities.
This is disabled because publishing interactive activities requires a subscription.
This book does not have any games.
This is disabled because this book does not have any canvas elements that qualify as “comic-like”, such as speech bubbles.
Select this if you want to list this book as a Comic Book. This only affects how the book is categorized, it does not affect the content of the book.
This is disabled because this book does not have any pages with motion enabled.
Select this if you want to show motion when the book is read in landscape mode.
This is disabled because this book does not have any videos.
The videos in this book contain {N}
trans-unit.note: {N} will be replaced with a link whose text is the name of the (sign) language and which opens a chooser

trans-unit.source: This is disabled because this collection has {no sign language selected}.
trans-unit.note: The result should have a section in braces. This will become a hot link which will open a user interface that lets the user select a sign language.

trans-unit.source: This is disabled because this book does not have any Talking Book recordings.
This is disabled because no “Talking Book Languages” are selected.
This is disabled because this book does not have any image descriptions in the primary language.
Select this if you want to list this book as accessible to the visually impaired in the primary language. This only affects how the book is categorized, it does not affect the content of the book.
Do not show this book to the public yet. I will share its URL with reviewers for feedback.
Consider whether you can distribute your book using the BloomPUB format instead of, or in addition to, the ePUB format.
[Some Bloom features] are not supported by most or all ePUB readers.
trans-unit.note: The text inside the [square brackets] will become a link to a website.

trans-unit.source: Most ePUB readers are very low quality ([see our research and recommendations]).
trans-unit.note: The text inside the [square brackets] will become a link to a website.
"Linux" is an operating system name which may not translate.

trans-unit.source: This feature is available only on the Windows version of Bloom. We apologize for the inconvenience.
trans-unit.note: "Windows" and "Linux" are operating system names which may not translate.

trans-unit.source: Why you should publish on the Web
trans-unit.note: This phrase has a link to an explanation of the benefits of sharing books on BloomLibrary.org.
This phrase has a link to bring up the help viewer on the topic "Publish to Web".
This phrase has a link to the "About Bloom" page on that website.
Menu command (and dialog title) that imports one or more books from .bloomSource files into the current collection. Keep ".bloomSource" unchanged; it is a file extension.

Title of the dialogs shown while importing .bloomSource file(s): the one that asks whether to import for editing or as derivatives, and the one that asks how to handle books already in the collection.
Radio-button choice in the import dialog: import the book(s) to edit them directly.
Explanation shown under the "Import for editing" radio-button choice in the import dialog.

trans-unit.source: Import as derivatives — new books based on the originals
trans-unit.note: Radio-button choice in the import dialog: use the imported book(s) as the basis for new derivative books (e.g. translations).

trans-unit.source: Use a new book ID & make room for new copyright and credits while preserving those of the original.
trans-unit.note: Explanation shown under the "Import as derivatives" radio-button choice in the import dialog.

trans-unit.source: Some of these books are already in this collection.
trans-unit.note: Shown once when importing .bloomSource file(s), if any of the books is already in the collection.
Radio-button choice in the import duplicate prompt: replace the existing book(s) with the imported one(s).

trans-unit.source: The current copies will be overwritten.
trans-unit.note: Secondary line under the "Replace the existing books" choice in the import duplicate prompt.

trans-unit.source: All books must be checked out
trans-unit.note: Secondary line under the "Replace the existing books" choice in the import duplicate prompt, shown (and the choice disabled) when the collection is a Team Collection and one or more of the books being replaced is not checked out.
Radio-button choice in the import duplicate prompt: keep the existing book(s) and add the imported one(s) as new copies.

trans-unit.source: The existing books stay as they are.
trans-unit.note: Secondary line under the "Add them as new copies" choice in the import duplicate prompt.
Title of the progress dialog shown while .bloomSource file(s) are being imported.
{0} is the name of a Branding pack
{0} is the name of an xmatter pack
Area label for tabs/pages that affect all pages in the current book.

trans-unit.source: Book settings apply to all of the pages of the current book.
trans-unit.note: Description text shown for the Book area in the combined Book and Page Settings dialog.
Area label for tabs/pages that affect only the current page.

trans-unit.source: Page settings apply to the current page.
trans-unit.note: Description text shown for the Page area in the combined Book and Page Settings dialog.
Label for the page-level Colors page within the combined Book and Page Settings dialog.
keyword used to describe the size in pixels of a display screen or of an image
label for our small resolution (600 x 600)
abbreviation for Full High Definition (1920 x 1080)
abbreviation for Ultra High Definition, also known as "4K" (3480 x 2160)

trans-unit.source: Bloom reduces images to a maximum size to make books easier to view over poor internet connections and take up less space on phones.
trans-unit.note: displayed below the slider that is used to set the image resolution

trans-unit.source: Fill the front cover with a single image

Using this option turns on the [Print Bleed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bleed_%28printing%29) indicators on paper layouts. See [Full Page Cover Images](https://docs.bloomlibrary.org/full-page-cover-images) for information on sizing your image to fit.
Replace the front cover content with a single full-bleed image. See [Full Page Cover Images](https://docs.bloomlibrary.org/full-page-cover-images) for information on sizing your image to fit.

Enable full bleed layout for printing. This turns on the [Print Bleed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bleed_%28printing%29) indicators on paper layouts. See [Full Bleed Layout](https://docs.bloomlibrary.org/full-bleed) for more information.

trans-unit.note: obsolete as of Bloom 6.4, replaced by BookSettings.ThemeAndLayoutGroupLabel
Main section label in the Book Settings dialog that contains print-related options such as Full Bleed.

trans-unit.source: Languages to show in normal text boxes
Page Themes are a bundle of margins, borders, and other page settings. For information about each theme, see [Page Themes Catalog](https://docs.bloomlibrary.org/page-themes-catalog).

trans-unit.note: Show Credits on the cover page
Indicates page number location should be chosen automatically
Indicates page number should be positioned on the left side of the page
Indicates page number should be centered
Indicates page number should be positioned on the right side of the page
Indicates page numbers should not be visible

trans-unit.source: Note: some Page Themes may not know how to change the location of the Page Number.
Normally, books use the front & back matter pack that is chosen for the entire collection. Using this setting, you can cause this individual book to use a different one.

trans-unit.note: This term tries to briefly describe the native size of the image in pixel dimensions.
{0} is a placeholder for a filename and should not be translated

trans-unit.source: The {0} stylesheet of this book is incompatible with modern themes. Bloom is using it because the book is using the Legacy-5-6 theme. Click [here] for more information.
trans-unit.note: {0} is a placeholder for a filename. The text inside the [square brackets] will become a link to a website.

trans-unit.source: Bloom found a known version of {0} in this book and replaced it with a modern theme. You can delete it unless you still need to publish the book from an earlier version of Bloom.
trans-unit.note: {0} is a placeholder for a filename.

trans-unit.source: The {0} stylesheet of this book is incompatible with modern themes. Bloom is currently ignoring it. If you don't need those customizations any more, you can delete your {0}. Click [here] for more information.
trans-unit.note: {0} is a placeholder for a filename. (Note that it appears twice.) The text inside the [square brackets] will become a link to a website.

trans-unit.source: The {0} stylesheet of this book is incompatible with modern themes. Bloom is currently ignoring it. Click [here] for more information.
trans-unit.note: {0} is a placeholder for a filename. The text inside the [square brackets] will become a link to a website.

trans-unit.source: The selected page theme does not support the following settings.
The selected [Page Theme] does not support the following settings.
trans-unit.note: When you translate, make sure to include some text in between square brackets. Those become the clickable link that opens Book and Page Settings.

trans-unit.source: This feature requires a newer [Page Theme].
trans-unit.note: When you translate, make sure to include some text in between square brackets. Those become the clickable link that opens Book and Page Settings.

trans-unit.source: Extra space between pages near the book spine. Increase this for books with many pages to ensure text isn't lost in the binding. This gap is applied to each side of the spine.
Smart spacing around text boxes. Works well for simple pages, but may not suit custom layouts.
When you publish a book to the web or as an ebook, Bloom will flag any problematic fonts. For example, we cannot legally host most Microsoft fonts on BloomLibrary.org.
The Bloom file for this book also has some text in other languages. Please ignore any warnings here unless you plan to publish the book with these languages.
The following table shows where fonts have been used.
Font is not installed on this computer

trans-unit.note: flags that the same font is used for all languages for the given style
Do not change {0}. It will be replaced by the page number.

trans-unit.source: Customized Front and Back Matter Page Layout

trans-unit.note: Title of the dialog where the user chooses which books to include in the Android app.
Button label that opens the app settings dialog for the current Android app project.
Shown below the Customize button when required app settings are missing or invalid. %0 is a comma-separated list of field labels such as App Name or Package Name.
Button label for the first step that prepares the Android app project and required tools.
Label for a setup checklist step that installs Android build tools.
Label for a setup checklist step that downloads or locates the Reading App Builder installer.
Label for a setup checklist step that creates the Reading App Builder project for this collection.
Label for a setup checklist step that runs the Reading App Builder installer.
Label for a setup checklist step that creates the Android signing key.
Progress message shown while Bloom checks for a connected Android phone.
Progress message shown while Bloom installs the generated Android app on a connected phone.
Progress message shown while Bloom launches the installed Android app on a connected phone.
Title of the dialog used to edit Android app settings for the current collection.
Group heading inside the app settings dialog for the basic required fields.
Group heading inside the app settings dialog for appearance-related settings such as color scheme and icon.
Field label for the app name shown to end users on the Android device.
Field label for choosing the app's color scheme.
Field label for the Android package identifier. This is a technical identifier, not the user-visible app name.
Field label for the app icon chooser.
Label for the current custom icon when it is not one of the bundled icon choices.
Field label for the copyright text shown in the app.
Field label for the descriptive About text shown in the app.
Button label that resets the app icon to the default choice.
Title or accessible label for the app icon chooser.

trans-unit.source: Template: org.<yourorg>.<language-code>.<appname>. E.g. org.sil.xyz.stories. For additional help please contact the Digital Publishing Department of your organization.
trans-unit.note: Help text explaining how to form a valid Android package name. Keep the example package names lowercase and dot-separated.
Validation message shown when the package name field is blank.

trans-unit.source: Use a lowercase dot-separated package name with no spaces, like org.sil.sample.
trans-unit.note: Validation message shown when the Android package name is invalid. Keep the example package name lowercase and dot-separated.
Name of the external SIL application "Reading App Builder". Usually leave the product name unchanged.
Label for the section that opens Reading App Builder for additional app customization.
Progress text shown while Bloom launches the external Reading App Builder application.

trans-unit.source: Use this to do more customization of your app. Make sure to click Save and quit Reading App Builder before returning to Bloom.
trans-unit.note: Description for the button that opens Reading App Builder. "Reading App Builder" is the product name.
Button label that opens Windows File Explorer to show the generated APK file.
Heading shown at the top of the Publish to Apps screen.
Label for the Publish tab section used to build Android apps.

trans-unit.source: Create an app that you can install on your Android phone, share with others, and publish on the Google Play Store.
trans-unit.note: Tooltip or description for the Publish to Apps section. "Google Play Store" is a product name.
Button label for building a new Android APK.

trans-unit.source: The APK is current. Try on phone will use the latest build.
trans-unit.note: Tooltip shown when the build step is already up to date.

trans-unit.source: Run Prepare before building the app.
trans-unit.note: Tooltip shown when the user must run Prepare before Build.

trans-unit.source: Build a new APK from the current settings and selected books.

trans-unit.note: Button label that opens the dialog for choosing which books go into the Android app.

trans-unit.source: Choose which books to include in the app and the order they should appear.
trans-unit.note: Tooltip for the Choose Books button.
Progress message shown while Reading App Builder is building the Android APK.
Progress label shown when the current action is complete.
Progress message shown while Bloom creates the Reading App Builder project.
Progress message shown while Bloom exports BloomPUB files for the app build.
Progress message shown while the Android APK is being finalized.
Progress message shown while Bloom creates the Android signing key.
Progress message shown at the start of an Android app build.
Progress message shown while Bloom prepares the local workspace for app building.
Progress message shown while Bloom updates the existing Reading App Builder project.
Link text that opens the Reading App Builder website. "Reading App Builder" is the product name.

trans-unit.source: Edit the Reading App Builder app settings for this collection.
Run Prepare before using this step.
trans-unit.note: Tooltip shown when a later step is disabled until Prepare has been run.

trans-unit.source: The Reading App Builder project is already prepared for this collection.
trans-unit.note: Tooltip shown when the Prepare step is already complete.

trans-unit.source: Create the Reading App Builder project in this collection's Bloom App Data folder.
trans-unit.note: Tooltip for Prepare. "Bloom App Data" is the name of the collection-owned data folder.
Button label that installs the generated Android app on a connected phone and launches it.

trans-unit.source: Load and run the app on your phone. First enable USB Debugging on the phone and connect it with a USB cable.
trans-unit.note: Tooltip for the Try on phone button. "USB Debugging" is the Android setting name and may need to stay recognizable.
Summary text for the actual built app size. %0 is a formatted file size such as 82 MB.
Label for the non-book portion of the estimated app size.

trans-unit.source: This estimate is above the %0 Android app size limit. Choose fewer books or smaller books.
trans-unit.note: Warning shown when the estimated app size exceeds the Android size limit. %0 is the size limit, for example 100 MB.

trans-unit.source: Apps have a maximum size of about %1. The current estimate for this app is %0.
trans-unit.note: Summary text for the estimated total app size. %0 is the current estimated app size and %1 is the app size limit, for example 100 MB.
